page: Vultr voc-c-2c-4gb-50s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 2 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor running at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server provides 2 physical cores with a single thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled as a 50 GB local NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this configuration.

Benchmark results reveal a contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. In stress-ng single-core CPU testing, the instance achieves a top-tier rating in the top 10% with a score of 3434.78, and its single-threaded bzip3 decompression performance also reaches the top 10% tier at 14.65 GB/sec. However, multi-core stress-ng performance drops to the middle 50% average tier at 6819.14. Passmark CPU and physics scores fall into the bottom 25% weak category, reflecting the shared nature of the CPU allocation. Memory performance is mixed, with small-block cached read bandwidth scoring in the bottom 25%, while large-block read and write bandwidths maintain average middle 50% ratings.

The primary tradeoff of this instance lies in its shared CPU allocation, which limits sustained multi-threaded workloads and database operations, as evidenced by the weak Passmark database and Redis SET performance. Static web serving throughput is also low, placing in the bottom 10% tier. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a low-cost entry point for workloads that benefit from strong single-threaded burst performance but do not require dedicated CPU cores. This profile is suitable for lightweight background tasks, basic single-threaded file processing, and low-traffic utility services.
description: Vultr voc-c-2c-4gb-50s-amd is an entry-level instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 2 shared vCPUs on an AMD EPYC Genoa processor clocked at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of RAM, a bundled 50 GB local NVMe SSD, and one public IPv4 address. Performance benchmarks show a significant divergence: single-core capabilities are strong, with stress-ng single-core and single-threaded bzip3 decompression reaching top-tier levels. Conversely, multi-core performance and database operations fall into average or weak categories due to the shared CPU allocation. Memory bandwidth is average for large blocks but weak for small cached blocks. This server represents a budget-friendly option for workloads requiring bursty single-threaded compute rather than sustained multi-threaded execution, making it suitable for lightweight utility tasks, basic file processing, and non-production testing.
og_description: Vultr voc-c-2c-4gb-50s-amd is a shared-CPU instance featuring 2 AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and a 50 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core but weak multi-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-c-2c-4gb-50s-amd features 2 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and a 50 GB NVMe SSD for budget-friendly, single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly shared-CPU instance pairing strong single-threaded AMD Genoa performance with local NVMe storage for lightweight utility workloads.
bullet_points: 2 shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor at 2.0 GHz
4.0 GB of system memory and 50 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded decompression performance
Shared CPU allocation resulting in lower multi-core and database efficiency
Suitable for lightweight utility tasks, single-threaded processing, and testing
